result:
Now I have the complete trace. Here's the full path.

## Entry point

`SYSCALL_DEFINE3(read, unsigned int, fd, char __user *, buf, size_t, count)` — **fs/read_write.c:621**

## Macro expansion (x86_64-specific)

`SYSCALL_DEFINE3` → `SYSCALL_DEFINEx` (include/linux/syscalls.h:221,228) → `__SYSCALL_DEFINEx`. On x86_64 the arch header's `#ifndef __SYSCALL_DEFINEx` guard means the **x86 version wins**, defined at **arch/x86/include/asm/syscall_wrapper.h:228**. It expands the body at fs/read_write.c:621 into:

1. `__do_sys_read(fd, buf, count)` — the actual body, i.e. fs/read_write.c:622-624 (`return ksys_read(fd, buf, count);`)
2. `__se_sys_read(...)` — sign-extension wrapper (arch/x86/include/asm/syscall_wrapper.h:233), calls `__do_sys_read`
3. `__x64_sys_read(const struct pt_regs *regs)` — the real ABI entry point, generated by `__X64_SYS_STUBx` (arch/x86/include/asm/syscall_wrapper.h:96-98 / `__SYS_STUBx` at :74-80), which pulls args from `regs->di/si/dx` and calls `__se_sys_read(...)`

## Named function hops, in call order

| # | Function | File:line |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `do_syscall_64` (dispatches via `sys_call_table[nr](regs)`) | arch/x86/entry/common.c:73 (indexing at :50, inside inlined `do_syscall_x64`) |
| 2 | `__x64_sys_read(regs)` (decodes regs, calls `__se_sys_read`) | arch/x86/include/asm/syscall_wrapper.h:96-98 (generated for `read` via the macro invocation at fs/read_write.c:621) |
| 3 | `__se_sys_read(...)` (sign-extend wrapper) | arch/x86/include/asm/syscall_wrapper.h:233 |
| 4 | `__do_sys_read(fd, buf, count)` | fs/read_write.c:622 (body, opens at :621) |
| 5 | `ksys_read(fd, buf, count)` | fs/read_write.c:602 (call site :623) |
| 6 | `vfs_read(f.file, buf, count, ppos)` | fs/read_write.c:450 (call site :613) |

`vfs_read` itself then dispatches to `file->f_op->read` or `new_sync_read`/`file->f_op->read_iter` (fs/read_write.c:467-470), which is past the VFS boundary requested.

## Syscall-number → handler mapping

The in-tree (hand-maintained, non-generated) source of truth is **arch/x86/entry/syscalls/syscall_64.tbl:11**:
```
0	common	read			sys_read
```
Build-time scripts (`scripts/syscalltbl.sh` / `syscallhdr.sh`) turn this `.tbl` into the generated header `arch/x86/include/generated/asm/syscalls_64.h` (not in-tree source), which is `#include`d twice in **arch/x86/entry/syscall_64.c:11,17** — once to `extern`-declare `__x64_sys_read`, once to place it as element `sys_call_table[0]`. `do_syscall_64` (arch/x86/entry/common.c:73, indexing at :50) then indexes `sys_call_table[nr]` with the syscall number from `regs`, landing on `__x64_sys_read` for `nr == 0`.
